stem:
The table summarizes information about 40 community recreation centers, indicating whether each has a swimming pool, a gym, both, or neither.

If one of these recreation centers is selected at random, what is the probability that it has a swimming pool but does not have a gym?
rationale: Choice C is correct. According to the table, 10 of the 40 recreation centers have a pool but no gym. Therefore, the probability of randomly selecting such a center is 10/40 (which equals 1/4 or 0.25). Choice A is incorrect because 8/40 is the probability that a center has both a pool and a gym. Choice B is incorrect because 18/40 is the probability that a center has a pool (regardless of whether it has a gym). Choice D is incorrect because 12/40 is the probability that a center has a gym but no pool.
